summary: Item-system matching-improvement slice split out from PR #2617 for easier review. The PR touched seven files under src/melee/it, mostly small C reshapes for declaration/order/codegen: item collision ordering, Arwing laser absolute-value handling, Kyasarin helper argument passing, Link hookshot JObj access, Samus grapple hitbox/animation/link handling, Seak chain stick/velocity expression structure, and Seak needle thrown aliasing. The slice was reported as coming from a larger branch with no regressions before split, but review identified one local regression in itseakchain.c where replacing constant 1.0f with variable it_804DD0E0 hurt ordering.
what_changed: Split src/melee/it matching improvements from a larger branch/PR into a focused item-subsystem review slice.
Reordered locals in it_802706D0 in itcoll.c, including Item*, HSD_GObj*, HitCapsule*, and hit_index declarations, likely to affect stack/register ordering.
Changed itarwinglaser.c z-position absolute-value logic in it_802E72E0 by introducing z_abs and assigning z = -z_abs instead of mutating z through the absolute-value step.
Changed itKyasarin_FlipAndFall from fetching GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ internally to taking HSD_JObj* jobj and Item* ip as explicit arguments at call sites in it_802EDDC0.
Replaced direct link->gobj->hsd_obj access with GET_JOBJ(link->gobj) in two places in itlinkhookshot.c.
In itsamusgrapple.c, introduced a local struct spawn_hitbox_4* hitbox_data4 to shorten repeated data->create_hitbox.create_hitbox_4 accesses when filling HitCapsule fields.

In fn_802B7E34, replaced a samus_grapple_anim(gobj) call with its apparent inline expansion: refresh GET_ITEM, animate GET_JOBJ(gobj), walk ItemLink chain, and call HSD_JObjAnimAll on each link gobj hsd_obj.
In fn_802B8384, delayed initialization of link and fp locals until after declarations/PAD_STACK to adjust ordering.
In itseakchain.c, flattened inner stick_x/stick_y temporary blocks and accessed fp->fv.sk.lstick_delta.x/y directly in threshold/sign/multiply logic.
In itseakchain.c, removed vy_limit/vy_bias temporaries in favor of direct attrs->x18 and attrs->x28 expressions for vertical velocity wrap logic.
In itseakneedlethrown.c HitShield logic, introduced Item* ip2 alias and initialized attr at declaration; velocity writes and it_802762BC now use ip2.
smart_moves: Kept the PR scoped to src/melee/it so reviewers could evaluate item-only matching changes rather than a broad cross-subsystem diff.
Used tiny source-level reshapes rather than semantic rewrites: local declaration ordering, temporary introduction/removal, helper parameterization, and macro use.
Parameterized itKyasarin_FlipAndFall with already-needed GET_JOBJ/GET_ITEM results, a common decomp tactic when call-site evaluation/order matters.
Introduced hitbox_data4 in Samus grapple to control repeated nested struct access and likely improve register allocation or instruction ordering.
Expanded samus_grapple_anim inline in fn_802B7E34 where a helper call likely obscured the original function's code shape.
Used an alias pointer ip2 in Seak needle thrown to influence alias/register behavior without changing observed item velocity behavior.
decomp_lessons: For item matching, declaration order and initialization timing can matter even when C semantics are unchanged.
Inlining or un-inlining helpers should be treated as a matching tactic, not a readability-only decision; fn_802B7E34 shows helper expansion can be useful.
Replacing direct hsd_obj access with GET_JOBJ can be accepted when it preserves expected source conventions and codegen, but confirm macro expansion effects.
Introducing a pointer to a nested sub-struct, such as create_hitbox_4, can reduce repeated long access paths and alter generated code favorably.
Removing local float temporaries may improve one ordering case, but constants should not be replaced by variable globals if it causes regression.
PR slicing by directory/subsystem is useful for large matching-improvement branches because it isolates review and CI/decomp-dev reporting.
naming_conventions: Item object locals commonly use ip for Item* and gobj or item_gobj for Item_GObj*/HSD_GObj*.
JObj locals use jobj and should often be obtained via GET_JOBJ(gobj) when matching/conventions allow.
Fighter locals use fp and owner for the owning Fighter_GObj.
ItemLink traversal uses link, cur, iter, prev_link, head_link, and tail_link depending on role in the chain.
Temporary names such as z_abs and hitbox_data4 describe codegen-oriented aliases rather than new game concepts.
Existing autogenerated/unknown field names remain in use, such as xDD4_itemVar, x378_itemColl, x40_vel, xDCC_flag, x18, x28, x2C, and x43_b0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Reorder local declarations to affect stack layout/register allocation, as in it_802706D0 and it_802BAF2C.
Move initialization after PAD_STACK or after declarations to match original ordering, as in fn_802B8384.
Introduce temporaries for absolute-value handling when the compiler needs a separate value from the final signed result, as with z_abs in itarwinglaser.c.
Pass GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ values into static inline helpers rather than fetching inside them when call-site expression order affects matching.
Use sub-structure pointer aliases for nested data fields to alter address calculation and repeated load shape.
Inline a helper manually when the original function appears to contain the loop/calls directly, shown by replacing samus_grapple_anim with HSD_JObjAnimAll calls over the grapple link chain.
Use pointer aliases such as ip2 to influence register choice or aliasing in small item-state update functions.
Avoid swapping literal constants for global float variables solely for ordering; review flagged this as a regression for it_802A24D0(link, 1.0f).

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.comment: Regression - don't replace the constant with a variable for ordering. We can address this later.
review_feedback.evidence: Review comment on hunk changing it_802A24D0(link, 1.0f) to it_802A24D0(link, it_804DD0E0).

follow_up_queries: Check whether the review regression in itseakchain.c was fixed before merge or left for a later PR.
Search later PRs for it_802A24D0(link, 1.0f) versus it_804DD0E0 to see how the constant/global ordering issue was resolved.
Look for subsequent Samus grapple matching work around samus_grapple_anim, fn_802B7E34, and HSD_JObjAnimAll link traversal.
Compare item chain files using GET_JOBJ(link->gobj) versus direct link->gobj->hsd_obj to determine the preferred convention when matching.
